node: dgx3 (internal 8-GPU sm_80 datacenter server; hostname redacted)

gpus.name: 8x 80GB sm_80 datacenter GPU (model redacted)
gpus.arch: sm_80 (below the sm_90 DSA kernel floor)

storage_free._note: additional large home/scratch volumes present (identifiers redacted); /projects has ample room for the 466 GB shards

preflight_verdict.reason: sm_80 is below the DSA stock kernel floor (sm_90); stock SGLang/vLLM cannot serve GLM-5.2 here (vLLM #35021).

overcome.staging_dir: /projects/glm52-q4 (4.1 TB free; fits the 466 GB shards)
overcome.expert_offload: n-cpu-moe=999 keeps MoE experts on the ~2 TB host RAM; A100s hold attention/shared tensors
overcome.status_as_of_capture: runner committed to origin/main; launch blocked on a degraded private control bridge (box sessions stopped consuming stdin mid-session). Not a kernel/approach block; re-launch when the bridge recovers.

fak_path_verified_locally.note: The fak kernel + fak local guard path that will front the GLM-5.2 endpoint is verified end-to-end against an OpenAI-compatible wire (the same wire llama.cpp serves). Only the GLM-5.2 endpoint itself is gated on the box.
fak_path_verified_locally.fak_serve: answers OpenAI /v1/models and /v1/chat/completions; the kernel adjudicates the tool call on the wire (operation=adjudicate, verdict=ALLOW).
fak_path_verified_locally.fak_guard: `fak guard --provider openai --base-url <endpoint> -- <agent>` stands up an in-process kernel gateway, injects OPENAI_BASE_URL/OPENAI_API_BASE into the CHILD ONLY, enforces the capability floor on every proposed tool call, and writes a tamper-evident hash-chained audit journal (verify: fak audit verify <path>).
fak_path_verified_locally.remaining_variable: swap the guard --base-url from the local mock to http://<dgx3>:8000/v1 once tools/glm52_stage_serve_dgx3.sh reaches GLM52_SERVE_READY.

blocker.what: the live GLM-5.2 serve + `fak guard -- claude` end-to-end could NOT run this session.
blocker.cause: the DGX3 control bridge's box-side shell/PTY subsystem is hung: the control-hub bot is alive (answers !help/!sessions/!new, spawns sessions) but NO spawned shell executes input (no echo via !send, !new-inline, across kill+recreate, for multiple multi-minute watches). Not throttle (control commands reply); not a kernel/code/approach problem.
blocker.direct_ssh: closed for dgx3 (fleet_endpoints lists no dgx3 ssh; the lab path is ssh_port_open:false).
blocker.fix: out-of-band restart of the box-side slack-control session daemon (a human/box action), then run the resume commands in docs/serving/glm52-full-size-serving-witness.md §7. An auto-recovery watcher was left armed to fire the serve the moment the shell subsystem returns.

native_track_already_passing.note: fak's NATIVE in-kernel glm_moe_dsa forward already PASSES on this A100 class (sm_80), separate from external-engine serving.
